title: ROOT-CAUSE (over-match class): the 300s stop-review checkpoint runs the exhausted_regex against the RAW, unstripped pane — so agent-rendered content (a cat/grep/diff that merely SHOWS quota-wall text) can fast-fail a HEALTHY agent to exit 85. Strip agent output before the checkpoint exhaustion Observe, matching/exceeding the fast-poll mitigation.
summary: Distinct from (and the OPPOSITE of) exhaustion-regex-drift-failloud (which is UNDER-match: regex too narrow, misses a real wall). This is OVER-match: no exhausted_regex — however well-crafted — is fully safe while the checkpoint scans raw agent output, because the wall text can legitimately appear in the pane for a NON-wall reason (an agent reading/greping a file, test fixture, incident report, or diff that contains the wall string). Surfaced by the 2026-07-18 two-round go-review of the per-model quota-regex fix: the reviewer showed the committed inbox/test artifacts themselves contain wall text, and the queued follow-ups schedule agents to read exactly those files.
root_cause: Asymmetric mitigation across the two exhaustion-detection paths. The ~2s FAST-POLL path (go/internal/bridge/autorespond.go:333-341) runs stripPromptEchoLines before the ExhaustedOf check (and is documented to deliberately SKIP stripAgentDiffLines, scoping diff-stripping to prompt-matching only). But the 300s STOP-REVIEW CHECKPOINT (go/internal/bridge/driver_tmux_repl.go:669-679) calls livenessCenter.Observe(session, curPane, paneProfile) on the RAW captured pane with ZERO stripping; LivenessExhausted unconditionally wins Aggregate() (panestream/signalcenter.go:231-232) and returns ExitUnknownPrompt/85 immediately. So any exhausted_regex match on agent-rendered content at the checkpoint kills a healthy agent — the exhaustion override is also ungated by paneBusy (fires even while the CLI is visibly working). Precedent: cycle-314 (a test fixture typed 'You've hit your usage limit' benched codex), cycle-641/642/654/656 (an echoed Deliverable-Contract line benched a PASSING phase) — the strip functions exist BECAUSE of this class, but the checkpoint path never adopted them.
fix: The exhaustion determination must be made on CLI CHROME, not agent output. Options (design first): (1) run the same content-stripping the fast-poll path uses (prompt-echo + agent-diff/file-render lines) on the checkpoint pane BEFORE the Observe/Aggregate exhaustion check — the minimal parity fix; (2) require the exhausted_regex match to sit in a CLI-chrome region (adjacent to the REPL prompt marker / system-message box-drawing prefix the driver already knows), not anywhere in a multi-KB pane; (3) require two consecutive checkpoints to agree the wall persists (a transient echo of wall text in a scrolling pane clears; a real wall stays) before fast-failing. Prefer (1)+(3): strip, and require persistence, so a one-frame content echo cannot trip it. Config-driven where patterns are involved (manifest/policy single-source), zero Go literals for the patterns. This is the CLASS fix that makes every exhausted_regex branch (incl. the pre-existing legacy ones) safe against content-echo false-positives — the per-model regex tightening (companion-anchored) reduces the surface but does not close the class.
acceptance: A checkpoint pane whose ONLY exhausted_regex hit comes from agent-rendered content (a cat/grep/diff of a file containing wall text) does NOT fast-fail — exit is the normal ExitArtifactTimeout, not 85.
A genuine CLI-chrome quota wall (rendered by the CLI itself, adjacent to the prompt) STILL fast-fails to exit 85 (no regression of the real detection).
Parity: the checkpoint path and the fast-poll path apply the SAME content exclusion (single-source the stripping) — no third divergent scan.
Regression test: a fixture pane containing the real wall string as AGENT OUTPUT (e.g. a rendered file-read) is NOT treated as exhaustion; a fixture with the wall as CLI chrome IS.
files: go/internal/bridge/driver_tmux_repl.go:669-679 (checkpoint Observe on raw pane — the gap)
connects_to: audit-artifact-timeout-2wide (the per-model regex fix that surfaced this; companion-anchoring reduces but does not close this class)
exhaustion-regex-drift-failloud (the OPPOSITE failure mode — under-match drift diagnostic)
the cycle-314 / cycle-641-656 false-FAIL precedents that created the strip functions
notes: Fable-discipline framing: fail-open/echo-sensitive detectors are a bug factory (§2). A false-POSITIVE here kills a working agent (cardinal sin, cycle-254/255); a false-NEGATIVE merely fails over (safe) — so the design must bias hard toward not-matching agent content. The companion-anchored per-model regex (shipped in the audit-artifact-timeout branch) is the interim surface-reduction; THIS is the durable class fix.
